sections.heading: Definition
sections.content: A public corporation is a government-owned business established to provide essential services rather than to make profit.

sections.heading: Reasons for Establishment
sections.content: They are created to provide essential services, prevent exploitation, ensure security, and generate employment.

sections.heading: Problems
sections.content: Challenges include political interference, corruption, favoritism, inefficiency, and poor work attitude.

sections.heading: Control Measures
sections.content: Public corporations are controlled through parliamentary oversight, judicial review, ministerial supervision, and public criticism.
